codeReview.summary: This rerun fix adequately addresses the prior blocking issue from fix-deep-upsert-trigger-ordering. In quantdb/generic_ingest.py, _ensure_obj_desc_inst now checks for the exact (object, desc_inst) pair before returning, which matches the composite FK requirement on values_quant/values_cat. The new regression test in test/test_deep_upsert.py exercises the previously broken scenario by pre-creating an unrelated obj_desc_inst row and then deep_upserting a values_quant row for a different desc_inst, verifying that the second obj_desc_inst pair and corresponding obj_desc_quant row are auto-created. I found no remaining in-scope correctness issues in this fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared state does not currently document the values_quant trigger constraints that integration tests must satisfy, so the worker had to rediscover them while debugging the regression test.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl for session 74c3756d-3068-42d4-b10b-1eaf20d665c5 shows the first regression-test attempt failed on values_quant_check_before, after which the worker inspected sql/tables.sql:594-603 and ran ad hoc psql queries to find a values_inst row with matching desc_inst and a descriptors_quant row whose domain was NULL or the same desc_inst. Neither /Users/tmsincomb/Dropbox (Personal)/repos/quantitative-db/.factory/library/architecture.md nor AGENTS.md currently captures those trigger-specific test requirements.

summary: Pass: this fix closes the remaining blocking gap from fix-deep-upsert-trigger-ordering by ensuring _ensure_obj_desc_inst matches the specific (object, desc_inst) pair and by adding a regression test for the previously broken case with an existing unrelated obj_desc_inst row.
